message:
docs(providers): update canonical ID table, add auth crosswalk and redaction diagnostics

Update provider documentation artifacts:

- docs/provider-canonical-id-table.json: refresh with latest alias
  additions (nanogpt, novita, zhipu/glm)
- docs/provider-discrepancy-classification.json: update behavioral
  discrepancy classifications with recent parity findings
- docs/provider-auth-crosswalk.json (new, 244 lines): maps provider
  canonical IDs to their authentication mechanisms, env var chains,
  and credential format expectations for diagnostic tooling
- docs/provider-auth-redaction-diagnostics.json (new, 302 lines):
  catalog of auth-related error response patterns with redaction rules
  for safe logging — prevents API keys and tokens from leaking into
  diagnostic output

Also syncs .beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,244 @@
+{
+  "schema": "pi.docs.provider_auth_crosswalk.v1",
+  "bead_id": "bd-3uqg.9.3.2",
+  "generated_at_utc": "2026-02-14T00:40:00Z",
+  "generated_by": "LilacCat (Claude Opus 4.6)",
+  "description": "Canonical/alias/env-key auth crosswalk covering all 87 Pi providers. Includes env key precedence, base URL defaults/overrides, and high-confusion mappings with test evidence references.",
+
+  "api_key_resolution_precedence": {
+    "description": "How Pi resolves API keys for any provider (src/auth.rs resolve_api_key())",
+    "steps": [
+      {"priority": 1, "source": "Explicit override parameter (--api-key CLI flag or programmatic override)", "note": "Highest priority, always wins"},
+      {"priority": 2, "source": "Environment variables (checked in order from auth_env_keys list)", "note": "First match wins; e.g., for google: GOOGLE_API_KEY then GEMINI_API_KEY"},
+      {"priority": 3, "source": "auth.json file (~/.pi/agent/auth.json, keyed by canonical provider ID)", "note": "Persisted credentials from /login command"},
+      {"priority": 4, "source": "Canonical ID fallback (if alias used, looks up canonical's auth.json entry)", "note": "Ensures aliases share auth state"}
+    ],
+    "evidence": "src/auth.rs:320-353"
+  },
+
+  "auth_method_patterns": {
+    "bearer_token": {
+      "header": "Authorization: Bearer {API_KEY}",
+      "providers_count": 73,
+      "description": "Standard OpenAI-compatible pattern used by most providers"
+    },
+    "x_api_key": {
+      "header": "X-API-Key: {API_KEY}",
+      "providers_count": 1,
+      "providers": ["anthropic"],
+      "description": "Anthropic's custom auth header"
+    },
+    "api_key_header": {
+      "header": "api-key: {API_KEY}",
+      "providers_count": 1,
+      "providers": ["azure-openai"],
+      "description": "Azure OpenAI's custom auth header"
+    },
+    "url_parameter": {
+      "pattern": "?key={API_KEY}",
+      "providers_count": 1,
+      "providers": ["google"],
+      "description": "Google/Gemini passes key as URL parameter"
+    },
+    "anthropic_messages_api": {
+      "header": "X-API-Key: {API_KEY}",
+      "providers_count": 6,
+      "providers": ["kimi-for-coding", "minimax", "minimax-cn", "minimax-coding-plan", "minimax-cn-coding-plan", "zenmux"],
+      "description": "Anthropic Messages API proxies using X-API-Key pattern"
+    },
+    "aws_credential_chain": {
+      "mechanism": "IAM SigV4 signing (not API key)",
+      "providers_count": 1,
+      "providers": ["amazon-bedrock"],
+      "description": "AWS standard credential chain: env vars → profile → instance metadata"
+    },
+    "no_auth_required": {
+      "providers_count": 1,
+      "providers": ["ollama"],
+      "description": "Local inference server, no authentication needed"
+    }
+  },
+
+  "high_confusion_mappings": {
+    "description": "Provider name/alias mappings that commonly confuse users, per acceptance criteria",
+    "entries": [
+      {
+        "user_input": "azure",
+        "resolves_to": "azure-openai",
+        "mechanism": "Alias registered in provider_metadata.rs:1412",
+        "confusion_source": "Upstream surfaces use 'azure', Pi canonical is 'azure-openai'",
+        "env_key": "AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY",
+        "test_evidence": "cargo test canonical_provider_id -- azure"
+      },
+      {
+        "user_input": "azure-cognitive-services",
+        "resolves_to": "azure-openai",
+        "mechanism": "Alias registered in provider_metadata.rs:1412",
+        "confusion_source": "Legacy Azure service name still used in some docs",
+        "env_key": "AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY",
+        "test_evidence": "cargo test canonical_provider_id -- azure_cognitive"
+      },
+      {
+        "user_input": "fireworks-ai",
+        "resolves_to": "fireworks",
+        "mechanism": "Alias registered in provider_metadata.rs:251",
+        "confusion_source": "Official company name is 'Fireworks AI' but Pi uses short 'fireworks'",
+        "env_key": "FIREWORKS_API_KEY",
+        "test_evidence": "cargo test canonical_provider_id -- fireworks"
+      },
+      {
+        "user_input": "qwen",
+        "resolves_to": "alibaba",
+        "mechanism": "Alias registered in provider_metadata.rs",
+        "confusion_source": "Qwen is the model family, Alibaba/DashScope is the provider",
+        "env_key": "DASHSCOPE_API_KEY or QWEN_API_KEY",
+        "test_evidence": "cargo test canonical_provider_id -- qwen"
+      },
+      {
+        "user_input": "kimi",
+        "resolves_to": "moonshotai",
+        "mechanism": "Alias registered in provider_metadata.rs",
+        "confusion_source": "Kimi is the product name, Moonshot AI is the provider. Note: kimi-for-coding is a SEPARATE provider",
